slides.title: City Places
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 1 English, Unit 9: City Places. In this unit, your child learns the words for places in a town — the shop, the market, the school, the hospital, the mosque. They learn the people who work in these places, and simple words that tell us where things are (near, far, next to). They also meet the traffic lights and learn that red means stop and green means go. The big idea is that a town is full of helpful places and kind people, and that we all help keep our town clean and safe. The goal is warm, confident English — not perfect spelling. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Name places in a town (shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park)
Name some people who help us (teacher, doctor, shopkeeper)
Say where a place is using position words (near, next to, here, there)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to name places in a town (shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park). you will also name some people who help us (teacher, doctor, shopkeeper). you will also say where a place is using position words (near, next to, here, there). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: shop, market, school, hospital, mosque, park. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes A Walk Around Town and Talk about City Places. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Language pattern 1
bullets: Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: There is a ___.
slides.narration: The first language focus is Language pattern 1. Listen to the model, point to the matching picture, then say the pattern: There is a ___. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 - Listen and point. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
